issue.body:
**Work-readiness:** AFK

## Purpose

This is the tracer-bullet foundation for the whole epic (#44): the transactional-outbox delivery seam, proven end-to-end on one standalone event before any claims data depends on it. It realises the delivery mechanism in [ADR-008](docs/adr/adr008-canonical-claim-store-transactional-outbox.md) — a write commits its canonical change *and* an `outbox` row in one transaction, a best-effort post-commit send delivers it to Inngest, and a cron sweep re-delivers anything that didn't make it. Everything else in the epic is a write-site that reuses this seam.

## Current behaviour

No outbox, no Inngest. There is no durable mechanism to schedule a side effect after a DB write. `inngest` is not a dependency; `src/inngest/` and the `/api/inngest` route do not exist; `vercel.json` is absent.

## Desired behaviour

- An `outbox` table exists with the ADR-008 shape: `id` (UUID PK, generated in JS, doubles as the Inngest event id), `event_name`, `payload` (JSONB), `created_at`, `processed_at` (NULL = undelivered — the sole delivery-state sentinel), `attempts`, `last_error`. A partial index covers `(processed_at, created_at) WHERE processed_at IS NULL`. No `status` enum.
- A typed outbox module exposes two helpers:
  - `buildOutboxEvent(name, payload)` → `{ id, name, data, insert }` where `insert` is an unexecuted Drizzle insert ready to drop into a `db.batch`. The payload is validated against a Zod event registry *before* insert; an unregistered name or malformed payload is a compile error **and** a runtime rejection.
  - `sendPostCommit(events)` → best-effort: on send success stamps `processed_at` (guarded `WHERE processed_at IS NULL`); on failure logs (prefixed `[outbox]`) and **swallows** — never throws to the caller.
- A typed Inngest client is registered via `EventSchemas.fromZod(...)` over a Zod event registry (the registry starts with one tracer event; the claim events arrive in later slices). The SDK reads `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` / `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY` from env, not the constructor.
- The `/api/inngest` route serves the client + function registry with `maxDuration = 300`.
- An **outbox sweep** cron (`0 * * * *`) selects `processed_at IS NULL AND created_at < now() - interval '30 seconds'` ORDER BY `created_at` LIMIT 100, and per row re-sends (each its own memoised `step.run`) then stamps `processed_at`, or increments `attempts` + records `last_error`. The sweep logic is a pure `runSweep(step)` function for testability.
- An **outbox prune** cron (`0 3 * * *`) deletes `processed_at IS NOT NULL AND processed_at < now() - interval '7 days'`. Unprocessed rows are never auto-deleted — they stay operator-visible. Pure `runPrune(step)`.
- The local Inngest dev server is wired into the dev flow so post-commit sends and crons hit it immediately (the project runs portless — wire the dev server against the portless URL; check how `scripts/dev.sh` currently launches the app and extend it).
- Env keys `INNGEST_EVENT_KEY` / `INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY` are added to the env schema as **optional**.

## Key interfaces

- `buildOutboxEvent(name, payload)` — generic over the Zod event-registry keys; returns `{ id, name, data, insert }`.
- `sendPostCommit(events)` — `Promise<void>`, swallows on send failure, stamps `processed_at` on success.
- `runSweep(step)` / `runPrune(step)` — pure functions taking a `step` shim, callable in tests with a mock `step`.
- Event registry — a Zod schema per event name, registered via `EventSchemas.fromZod(...)`.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `outbox` table created via `make db_generate` → `make db_migrate` (NOT `drizzle-kit push`) with the partial index on `(processed_at, created_at) WHERE processed_at IS NULL`.
- [ ] `buildOutboxEvent` rejects a malformed payload at runtime (Zod) and an unregistered event name fails to type-check.
- [ ] `sendPostCommit` stamps `processed_at` on send success and, on a forced send failure, swallows the error and leaves `processed_at` NULL (unit test asserts both).
- [ ] One standalone tracer event flows end-to-end on the local Inngest dev server: written to `outbox` in a `db.batch`, delivered post-commit, `processed_at` stamped.
- [ ] `runSweep(step)` re-delivers an unprocessed row > 30s old and stamps it; on send failure increments `attempts` and records `last_error` (unit test with a mock `step`).
- [ ] `runPrune(step)` deletes a processed row older than 7 days and leaves an unprocessed row untouched (unit test).
- [ ] `/api/inngest` route serves the client + functions with `maxDuration = 300`.
- [ ] `make check`, `make build`, and `make test_app` pass (run via @agent-codebase-verification).

## Out of scope

- The `claims` and child tables, and any claim event — slice #46 (schema) and #47 (intake→assessment).
- The assessment and comms-dispatch workers — slices #47 and #49.
- Prod Inngest Cloud provisioning + Terraform secrets (epic out-of-scope; design §9 follow-on).
- Changing `vercel.json` — crons are Inngest-native; `vercel.json` stays absent / `{}`.

## References

- Design: `thoughts/designs/2026-06-22-transactional-outbox-inngest-foundation.md` §4.1, §5, §6, §7.3, §7.4, §9
- ADR: `docs/adr/adr008-canonical-claim-store-transactional-outbox.md` (Accepted)
- CLAUDE.md § Database Migrations (use `make db_generate`/`make db_migrate`, never `drizzle-kit push`) and § Workflows (verify via @agent-codebase-verification)
- Reference implementation: sibling `rekurve` project's outbox table + sweep/prune (copy the hardened pattern; this slice improves on it with typed payloads)
- Representative files (starting points — Inngest dir is greenfield): `src/server/db/schema.ts`, `src/env.js`, `scripts/dev.sh`

Part of #44

milestone.title: M1.5 — Production assessor build
milestone.description: Epic #19: router + doctrine prompt v2 + Bedrock re-baseline (design 2026-06-11)

issue.title: Add transactional-outbox tracer: outbox table + typed module + Inngest client/route/sweep/prune
